content.text:
## Summary

I successfully fixed the bug in the jsoup library related to handling of spurious Byte Order Marks (BOM) when no charset is set. Here's what I did:

### The Problem
The test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMarkWhenNoCharsetSet` was failing because the BOM detection and removal logic was not handling the case when no charset was explicitly set properly. The BOM check was happening after the document was already parsed with the BOM still in the content.

### The Root Cause
In the `parseByteData` method in `DataUtil.java`, when `charsetName` is `null`, the document gets created with `doc = parser.parseInput(docData, baseUri)` before the BOM check happens. The BOM check does remove the BOM from `docData`, but since `doc` is no longer `null`, the document is not re-parsed with the corrected content. This meant the BOM remained in the parsed document.

### The Solution
I moved the BOM detection and removal logic to occur immediately after the initial decoding and before any document parsing happens. This ensures that:

1. When `charsetName` is `null`, the BOM is checked and removed before parsing the document
2. When `charsetName` is not `null`, the BOM is still checked and handled appropriately
3. The BOM handling happens consistently regardless of the code path taken

The fix involved moving the BOM detection code block from after the document creation to immediately after the initial decoding in both branches of the conditional (`if (charsetName == null)` and `else`).

### Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMarkWhenNoCharsetSet` now passes
- The related test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMark` still passes
- The project compiles successfully
- The generated patch is in standard diff format and ready for application

The solution maintains the intended behavior that "UTF-8 BOM indicator takes precedence over everything else" as stated in the code comment.

result:
## Summary

I successfully fixed the bug in the jsoup library related to handling of spurious Byte Order Marks (BOM) when no charset is set. Here's what I did:

### The Problem
The test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMarkWhenNoCharsetSet` was failing because the BOM detection and removal logic was not handling the case when no charset was explicitly set properly. The BOM check was happening after the document was already parsed with the BOM still in the content.

### The Root Cause
In the `parseByteData` method in `DataUtil.java`, when `charsetName` is `null`, the document gets created with `doc = parser.parseInput(docData, baseUri)` before the BOM check happens. The BOM check does remove the BOM from `docData`, but since `doc` is no longer `null`, the document is not re-parsed with the corrected content. This meant the BOM remained in the parsed document.

### The Solution
I moved the BOM detection and removal logic to occur immediately after the initial decoding and before any document parsing happens. This ensures that:

1. When `charsetName` is `null`, the BOM is checked and removed before parsing the document
2. When `charsetName` is not `null`, the BOM is still checked and handled appropriately
3. The BOM handling happens consistently regardless of the code path taken

The fix involved moving the BOM detection code block from after the document creation to immediately after the initial decoding in both branches of the conditional (`if (charsetName == null)` and `else`).

### Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMarkWhenNoCharsetSet` now passes
- The related test `org.jsoup.helper.DataUtilTest::discardsSpuriousByteOrderMark` still passes
- The project compiles successfully
- The generated patch is in standard diff format and ready for application

The solution maintains the intended behavior that "UTF-8 BOM indicator takes precedence over everything else" as stated in the code comment.
